summary: Completed comprehensive testing of 'Best Matches For You' collection page for Unchained Coffee Taste Fit MVP. Backend APIs fully functional (25/25 tests passed including new batch taste-fit endpoint). Collection page successfully displays all 6 products with dynamic personalization based on user profile. Features working: default/personalized hero sections, score badges, rank labels, filtering, sorting, navigation, and per-attribute breakdowns.
passed_tests: Backend batch taste-fit API returns scores for all 6 products
Collection page loads at /demo/collection with all 6 products displayed
Default hero shows 'Our Coffees' when no profile exists
Personalized hero shows 'Best Matches For Your Palate' when profile exists
Filter by Process dropdown works (Natural, Honey, Washed, Semi-Washed, Anaerobic Natural)
Filter by Roast dropdown works (Light, Medium, Dark, Light-Medium, Medium-Light)
Sort by Price Low to High works correctly
Sort by Price High to Low works correctly
Product cards link to PDP pages (/demo/pdp/:handle)
Score badges appear on all cards when profile exists (99%, 96%, 94%, etc.)
TOP PICK, #2 MATCH, #3 MATCH rank labels appear when profile exists
Products sorted by match score descending when profile exists
Per-attribute breakdown bars show on cards when profile exists
Sort dropdown shows 'Best Match' option only when profile exists
Navigation: Shop link in header goes to /demo/collection
Navigation: PDP has 'View all coffees' link back to collection
Product count displays correctly (6 coffees, sorted by taste fit when applicable)
All existing backend APIs still working (25/25 tests passed)
Home route (/) redirects to /demo/collection correctly
All 6 mock products display with correct data (3 original + 3 new products)
Batch taste-fit API processes all products and returns proper scores
Collection state switches correctly between default and personalized modes
success_percentage: Backend: 100% (25/25 tests passed), Frontend: 100% (all collection features working perfectly)
